bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts title field containing newline characters without validation
bugs.expected: Backend should reject recipe title containing control characters like newlines with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted the recipe with status 201 (Created), title was stored with embedded newlines
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with title: "Title\nWith\nNewlines", description: "desc", ingredients: [], steps: [], prepTime: 10, servings: 2, isPublic: false, groupIds: []
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - title validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation to reject titles containing control characters. Use regex: /[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/ to detect control chars. Check: if (/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/.test(title)) throw ValidationError("Title contains invalid control characters")

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts prepTime as decimal number instead of rejecting non-integer values
bugs.expected: Backend should reject prepTime values that are not integers with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted prepTime: 10.5 with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with prepTime: 10.5 (decimal instead of integer)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - prepTime validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check: if (!Number.isInteger(prepTime)) throw ValidationError("prepTime must be an integer"). Also validate >= 0.

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts negative values for servings field
bugs.expected: Backend should reject servings <= 0 with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted servings: -5 with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with servings: -5
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - servings validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add range check: if (servings <= 0) throw ValidationError("servings must be a positive integer")

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts invalid MongoDB ObjectId format in groupIds array without validation
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-ObjectId strings in groupIds with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted groupIds: ["notanobjectid"] with status 201 (Created), storing invalid reference
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with groupIds: ["notanobjectid"] (not a valid MongoDB ObjectId)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - groupIds validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Use MongoDB ObjectId.isValid(id) for each groupId: groupIds.forEach(id => { if (!ObjectId.isValid(id)) throw ValidationError("Invalid group ID format") })

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts string value for groupIds field instead of array
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-array groupIds with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted groupIds: "notanarray" with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with groupIds: "notanarray" (string instead of array)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - groupIds type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check before iteration: if (!Array.isArray(groupIds)) throw ValidationError("groupIds must be an array")

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts string value for ingredients field instead of array of objects
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-array ingredients with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted ingredients: "not an array" with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with ingredients: "not an array" (string instead of array)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - ingredients type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check: if (!Array.isArray(ingredients)) throw ValidationError("ingredients must be an array of objects")

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts ingredient objects missing required 'name' field
bugs.expected: Backend should reject ingredients lacking required fields with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted ingredient without name: [{ quantity: '1', unit: 'cup' }] with status 201
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with ingredients: [{ quantity: '1', unit: 'cup' }] (missing name field)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - ingredient schema validation loop
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate each ingredient object: ingredients.forEach(ing => { if (!ing.name || !ing.quantity || !ing.unit) throw ValidationError("Ingredient missing required fields (name, quantity, unit)") })

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts object value for steps field instead of array of strings
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-array steps with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted steps: { 0: 'step1' } with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with steps: { 0: 'step1' } (object instead of array)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - steps type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check: if (!Array.isArray(steps)) throw ValidationError("steps must be an array of strings")

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts string value 'true' for isPublic field instead of boolean
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-boolean isPublic with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted isPublic: "true" with status 201 (Created), stored as string
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with isPublic: "true" (string instead of boolean)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - isPublic type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check: if (typeof isPublic !== "boolean") throw ValidationError("isPublic must be a boolean value")

bugs.attack_description: Group endpoint accepts arbitrary strings as color instead of validating hex color format
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-hex-color strings with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted color: "not-a-color" with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with color: "not-a-color" (not a valid hex color)
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - color validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate color format with strict regex: const hexColorRegex = /^#([A-Fa-f0-9]{6}|[A-Fa-f0-9]{3})$/; if (!hexColorRegex.test(color)) throw ValidationError("color must be a valid hex color (e.g., #ff0000)")

bugs.attack_description: Group endpoint accepts hex color without # prefix, returns wrong status code
bugs.expected: Backend should require # prefix and reject bare hex with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend responded with status 409 (Conflict) instead of 400 (Bad Request)
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with color: "ff0000" (missing # prefix)
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - color validation and error handling
bugs.proposed_fix: Apply strict hex color regex requiring # prefix (see BUG-010). Return 400, not 409, for validation errors.

bugs.attack_description: Login endpoint returns 404 for invalid email format instead of 400 Bad Request
bugs.expected: Backend should return 400 Bad Request for invalid email format before checking database
bugs.actual: Backend returned 404 Not Found (suggests database lookup happened before validation)
bugs.reproduction: POST /auth/login with email: "'; DROP TABLE users; --" (invalid format, potential injection)
bugs.file_hint: Backend auth controller POST /auth/login - validation order
bugs.proposed_fix: Move input validation BEFORE database lookup. Validate email format first, return 400 immediately for invalid format. Only return 404 if validation passes but user not found.

bugs.attack_description: Recipe description field accepts control characters (tab, carriage return) without validation
bugs.expected: Backend should reject description containing control characters with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted description: "Desc with\ttab and\rcarriage" with status 201
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with description: "Desc with\ttab and\rcarriage"
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - description validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Apply same control character validation to description as title: if (/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/.test(description)) throw ValidationError(...)

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts servings: 0 (zero value)
bugs.expected: Backend should reject servings <= 0 with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted servings: 0 with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with servings: 0
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - servings range validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Change validation from servings < 0 to servings <= 0 (must be strictly positive)

bugs.attack_description: Group description field accepts newline characters without validation
bugs.expected: Backend should reject description with control characters with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted description: "Desc\nwith\nnewlines" with status 201
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with description: "Desc\nwith\nnewlines"
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - description validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add control character validation to description: if (/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/.test(description)) throw ValidationError(...)

bugs.attack_description: Group endpoint accepts null value for required color field
bugs.expected: Backend should reject null color with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend responded with status 409 (should be 400) when color is null
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with color: null
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - color null check and error handling
bugs.proposed_fix: Add null check early: if (!color || typeof color !== "string") throw ValidationError("color is required and must be a string")

bugs.attack_description: Recipe endpoint accepts ingredient with empty name string
bugs.expected: Backend should reject empty ingredient names with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted ingredient with name: '' (empty string) with status 201
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with ingredients: [{ name: '', quantity: '1', unit: 'cup' }]
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - ingredient name validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Check not only for missing field but also empty: ingredients.forEach(ing => { if (!ing.name || ing.name.trim() === '') throw ValidationError("Ingredient name cannot be empty") })

bugs.attack_description: Recipe accepts null values in steps array without validation
bugs.expected: Backend should reject null elements in steps array with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted steps: [null, 'step1'] with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with steps: [null, 'step1']
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - steps array element validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate each step element: steps.forEach(step => { if (typeof step !== "string" || !step.trim()) throw ValidationError("Each step must be a non-empty string") })

bugs.attack_description: Recipe accepts numeric quantity in ingredient instead of string
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-string ingredient quantity with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted ingredient: { name: 'flour', quantity: 2, unit: 'cups' } with status 201
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with ingredients: [{ name: 'flour', quantity: 2, unit: 'cups' }]
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - ingredient field type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate ingredient field types: ingredients.forEach(ing => { if (typeof ing.quantity !== "string" || typeof ing.unit !== "string") throw ValidationError(...) })

bugs.attack_description: Recipe accepts null unit in ingredient
bugs.expected: Backend should reject null ingredient unit with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted ingredient: { name: 'flour', quantity: '2', unit: null } with status 201
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with ingredients: [{ name: 'flour', quantity: '2', unit: null }]
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - ingredient unit validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add null check in ingredient validation: if (!ing.name || !ing.quantity || !ing.unit || typeof ing.unit !== "string") throw ValidationError(...)

bugs.attack_description: Recipe accepts numeric value for description field
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-string description with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted description: 789 with status 201 (Created)
bugs.reproduction: POST /recipes with description: 789 (number instead of string)
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller POST /recipes - description type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check: if (typeof description !== "string") throw ValidationError("description must be a string")

bugs.attack_description: Group accepts numeric value for description field
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-string description with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend accepted description: 999 with status 201 or 409
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with description: 999 (number instead of string)
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - description type validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check: if (typeof description !== "string") throw ValidationError("description must be a string")

bugs.attack_description: Group accepts array value for color field
bugs.expected: Backend should reject non-string color with 400 Bad Request
bugs.actual: Backend responded with status 409 when color: ['#ff0000'] (array)
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with color: ['#ff0000'] (array instead of string)
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - color type validation and error handling
bugs.proposed_fix: Add type check early: if (typeof color !== "string") throw ValidationError("color must be a string") (before attempting format validation)

bugs.attack_description: Group accepts emoji in color field when it should validate hex format strictly
bugs.expected: Backend should reject emoji with 400 Bad Request (hex color validation)
bugs.actual: Backend responded with 409 status code instead of 400
bugs.reproduction: POST /groups with color: '😀' (emoji)
bugs.file_hint: Backend group controller POST /groups - color format validation and error codes
bugs.proposed_fix: Use strict regex for hex validation and return 400 for all validation errors: const hexColorRegex = /^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/;

bugs.attack_description: Deleted user token remains valid and can be reused to access API endpoints
bugs.expected: 401 Unauthorized on all endpoints after user deletion
bugs.actual: 200 OK - Deleted user's token still works on GET /groups and other endpoints
bugs.reproduction:
1. POST /auth/register to create user A with token T
2. DELETE /users/me with token T
3. GET /groups with token T -> Returns 200 OK (should be 401)
bugs.file_hint: Backend auth middleware - likely missing user validation in JWT verification
bugs.proposed_fix: When DELETE /users/me is called, implement one of: (a) Add revocation list/blacklist with token jti, (b) Add 'deleted_at' timestamp to user and check it during auth, (c) Sign tokens with user password hash so password change/deletion invalidates all tokens

bugs.attack_description: POST /groups/reorder and POST /recipes/reorder accept foreign group/recipe IDs and return 200 OK without validation or error
bugs.expected: 403 Forbidden or 400 Bad Request when reordering endpoints receive IDs that don't belong to the current user
bugs.actual: 200 OK - Endpoint silently ignores foreign IDs instead of validating ownership
bugs.reproduction:
1. Create User A with Group GA and Group GB
2. Create User B with Group GC
3. User A POST /groups/reorder with body {ids: [GC]} -> Returns 200 (should be 403/400)
4. Verify User B's group GC order unchanged (was silently ignored)
bugs.file_hint: Backend POST /groups/reorder and POST /recipes/reorder endpoints
bugs.proposed_fix: Add ownership validation: Before reordering, query all entities by ID and verify userId matches authenticated user. Return 403 if any ID doesn't belong to user, or 400 if any ID not found. Example: 'SELECT userId FROM groups WHERE id IN (?) and check all have userId = ?'

bugs.attack_description: DELETE request to other user's group returns 404 instead of 403/401, revealing resource existence and enabling group enumeration
bugs.expected: 403 Forbidden or 401 Unauthorized (consistent with authorization failure)
bugs.actual: 404 Not Found - reveals that the group resource exists, only authorization issue
bugs.file_hint: DELETE /groups/:id endpoint
bugs.proposed_fix: Implement authorization check BEFORE database lookup. Return 403 Forbidden immediately if req.user.id !== group.userId, without querying database first. This prevents leaking whether resources exist.

bugs.attack_description: Can create recipe with non-existent or other user's groupIds, violating referential integrity constraints
bugs.expected: 400 Bad Request (validation error) when groupIds reference non-existent or unauthorized groups
bugs.actual: 201 Created - recipe persisted with orphaned groupIds that don't exist in database
bugs.reproduction:
1. User A creates group (grpA_id)
2. DELETE /groups/{grpA_id} (group deleted)
3. POST /recipes {title: 'Test', groupIds: [grpA_id]} 
4. Result: 201 Created
5. Recipe now has groupIds array containing non-existent group reference
6. Later attempts to retrieve/filter by that group may fail or return inconsistent data
bugs.file_hint: POST /recipes endpoint - missing FK validation before insert
bugs.proposed_fix: Before creating recipe, validate all groupIds exist and belong to current user: FOR EACH groupId IN request.groupIds: SELECT * FROM groups WHERE id = groupId AND userId = req.user.id. If any missing, return 400. Use transaction to ensure atomic validation.

bugs.attack_description: Can create recipe with non-existent groupIds from PATCH /recipes/:id update as well, violating FK integrity
bugs.expected: 400 Bad Request when updating recipe with non-existent groupIds
bugs.actual: 200 OK - recipe.groupIds updated with IDs that don't exist in groups table
bugs.file_hint: PATCH /recipes/:id endpoint - missing validation on groupIds update
bugs.proposed_fix: In PATCH /recipes/:id, before updating groupIds: validate all NEW groupIds exist and belong to current user. Reject if any group doesn't exist or isn't owned by user. Use same validation as POST /recipes.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with 1000 ingredients array
bugs.expected: Should reject with 400 (validation error for excessive ingredients)
bugs.actual: Accepts request with 201, stores all 1000 ingredients
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with body containing ingredients array of 1000 items. Each ingredient: {name, quantity, unit}. Backend accepts and stores all.
bugs.file_hint: backend API - recipe creation endpoint validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: ingredients.length must be <= 50 (or reasonable limit). Return 400 if exceeded.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with 1000 steps array
bugs.expected: Should reject with 400 (validation error for excessive steps)
bugs.actual: Accepts request with 201, stores all 1000 steps
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with body containing steps array of 1000 items. Backend accepts and stores all.
bugs.file_hint: backend API - recipe creation endpoint validation
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: steps.length must be <= 100 (or reasonable limit). Return 400 if exceeded.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with 2MB+1 image (exceeds 2MB limit)
bugs.expected: Should reject with 413 or 400 (payload too large)
bugs.actual: Accepts request with 201, stores oversized image (2MB+1 bytes)
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with image field set to 'data:image/jpeg;base64,' + 2MB+1 of base64 chars. Backend accepts and stores.
bugs.file_hint: backend API - recipe creation endpoint validation for image size
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: Check image size limit. Parse data URI and validate base64 decoded size <= 2MB. Return 400 if exceeded.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with malformed image data URI (missing base64 header)
bugs.expected: Should reject with 400 (invalid image format)
bugs.actual: Accepts request with 201, stores malformed data URI as-is
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with image field set to 'data:image/jpeg,notbase64' (no base64; prefix). Backend accepts.
bugs.file_hint: backend API - recipe creation endpoint validation for image format
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: For data URIs, check format is 'data:image/jpeg;base64,<valid-base64>'. Validate base64 is valid. Return 400 if not.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with invalid base64 characters in image
bugs.expected: Should reject with 400 (invalid base64)
bugs.actual: Accepts request with 201, stores invalid base64 string as-is
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with image field set to 'data:image/jpeg;base64,!!!invalid!!!' (non-base64 chars). Backend accepts.
bugs.file_hint: backend API - recipe creation endpoint validation for base64
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: Decode and validate base64 string. Return 400 if decoding fails.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with 10MB description causes 500 server crash
bugs.expected: Should reject with 413 or 400 (payload too large)
bugs.actual: Server returns 500 error - unhandled exception in request processing
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with description field set to 10MB string. Server returns: 'request entity too large' 500 error.
bugs.file_hint: backend API - express/nodejs body size limit configuration
bugs.proposed_fix: Set Express body size limit to reasonable value (e.g., 5MB). Add try-catch in route handlers to catch oversized payloads gracefully. Return 413 instead of 500.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with quantity field > 10 chars gets silently truncated
bugs.expected: Should reject oversized field with 400 or accept full value
bugs.actual: Silently truncates quantity field to 10 characters without warning
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with ingredient.quantity = '123456789012345' (15 chars). Response stores quantity as '1234567890' (truncated to 10).
bugs.file_hint: backend MongoDB schema for Recipe.ingredients[].quantity field - likely has maxlength 10
bugs.proposed_fix: Either: (1) Validate on input: quantity.length <= 10, return 400 if violated. (2) Increase schema limit to reasonable value (e.g., 50). (3) Document limit clearly.

bugs.attack_description: POST /recipes with unit field > 20 chars gets silently truncated
bugs.expected: Should reject oversized field with 400 or accept full value
bugs.actual: Silently truncates unit field to 20 characters without warning
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with ingredient.unit = 'cups_tablespoons_teaspoons' (26 chars). Response stores as 'cups_tablespoons_tea' (20 chars).
bugs.file_hint: backend MongoDB schema for Recipe.ingredients[].unit field - likely has maxlength 20
bugs.proposed_fix: Either: (1) Validate on input: unit.length <= 20, return 400 if violated. (2) Increase schema limit. (3) Document limit.

bugs.attack_description: Create recipe with negative prepTime (-30)
bugs.expected: Request should be rejected (400/422) - negative time is illogical
bugs.actual: Request accepted (201), recipe created with negative prepTime value
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with {prepTime: -30, servings: 2, ...}
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: prepTime must be >= 0 in POST /recipes and PATCH /recipes/:id handlers
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller - validation middleware
bugs.area: Backend API validation, data consistency, and input handling

bugs.attack_description: Create recipe with zero servings
bugs.expected: Request should be rejected (400/422) - zero servings is invalid
bugs.actual: Request accepted (201), recipe created with servings=0
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with {servings: 0, prepTime: 30, ...}
bugs.proposed_fix: Add validation: servings must be > 0 in recipe creation/update endpoints
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe controller - validation middleware
bugs.area: Backend API validation, data consistency, and input handling

bugs.attack_description: Reorder recipes with duplicate IDs [id1, id2, id1]
bugs.expected: Request should be rejected (400) - duplicate IDs are invalid
bugs.actual: Request accepted (200), operation completes without error
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes/reorder with {ids: [id1, id2, id1]}
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate ids array for duplicates: const hasDups = ids.length !== new Set(ids).size; if (hasDups) throw error
bugs.file_hint: Backend POST /recipes/reorder endpoint
bugs.area: Backend API validation, data consistency, and input handling

bugs.attack_description: Save recipe with non-existent groupId - backend silently filters it
bugs.expected: Either reject request (groupId must exist) OR fail if validation fails
bugs.actual: Request accepted (200), invalid groupId silently omitted from response, creating inconsistency
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes/{id}/save with {groupIds: ['000000000000000000000000']} where groupId doesn't exist
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate all groupIds exist and belong to user before saving: const invalid = groupIds.filter(gid => !userGroups.includes(gid)); if (invalid.length) throw ValidationError
bugs.file_hint: Backend POST /recipes/{id}/save endpoint
bugs.area: Backend API validation, data consistency, and input handling

bugs.attack_description: Store XSS payload in recipe title: '<script>alert("xss")</script>'
bugs.expected: Title should be sanitized/escaped or request rejected
bugs.actual: Payload stored as-is in database and returned in API responses without escaping
bugs.reproduction: POST /api/recipes with {title: '<script>alert("xss")</script>', ...}; then GET /api/recipes returns unescaped HTML
bugs.proposed_fix: Sanitize string inputs (title, description) using DOMPurify or similar. Frontend should also escape when rendering, but backend should be defensive
bugs.file_hint: Backend recipe creation and all text field endpoints - add sanitization middleware
bugs.area: Backend API validation, data consistency, and input handling

bugs.category: Unsafe property access without proper null guard
bugs.attack_description: Accessing recipe.author.name with incomplete guard check in ternary
bugs.expected: Recipe renders without accessing undefined properties, or shows fallback UI
bugs.actual: Code uses optional chaining in condition but accesses item.author.name without guarantee in JSX, causing crash if author is null
bugs.reproduction: Create a recipe with author field as null or undefined, view in RecipeListScreen - app crashes on render
bugs.file_hint: RecipeListScreen.tsx:122-123
bugs.proposed_fix: Change condition to explicit null check: `item.author && item.author.name ? <Text>por {item.author.name}</Text> : null`

bugs.category: Invalid Image URI rendering without validation
bugs.attack_description: Rendering Image component with undefined or empty URI
bugs.expected: Image either loads valid source or shows placeholder
bugs.actual: user.avatar could be undefined when passed to Image source={{ uri }} causing image load crash
bugs.reproduction: Login without avatar, navigate to profile - Image component may crash on render
bugs.file_hint: ProfileScreen.tsx:115-116
bugs.proposed_fix: Add explicit guard: `{user?.avatar ? <Image source={{ uri: user.avatar }} /> : <Text>{placeholder}</Text>}`

bugs.category: State update on unmounted component (race condition)
bugs.attack_description: Calling navigation.goBack() after async operation without mount check
bugs.expected: Navigation only occurs if component is still mounted
bugs.actual: handleSave calls navigation.goBack() after await without checking if component unmounted - causes memory leak warning
bugs.reproduction: Start saving recipe, immediately navigate back before save completes - React warning in console
bugs.file_hint: RecipeFormScreen.tsx:128-133
bugs.proposed_fix: Use useRef to track mount state: `const isMountedRef = useRef(true); useEffect(() => () => { isMountedRef.current = false }, []); in finally: if (isMountedRef.current) navigation.goBack()`

bugs.attack_description: Passing potentially invalid base64 or malformed image URIs to Image component
bugs.expected: Images load correctly or display placeholder
bugs.actual: recipe.image or recipe.images could contain invalid data URIs or corrupted base64 strings, causing Image component to error silently or crash
bugs.reproduction: Corrupt recipe.image field in AsyncStorage to invalid base64, navigate to RecipeDetail - image rendering fails
bugs.file_hint: RecipeDetailScreen.tsx:146, 190
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate URI format before rendering: `if (recipe.image && recipe.image.startsWith('data:image')) { <Image /> }`

bugs.attack_description: Rendering recipe.ingredients.map() without validating ingredients exists
bugs.expected: Component handles missing ingredients gracefully
bugs.actual: If recipe.ingredients is undefined or null, .map() throws 'Cannot read property map of undefined' error
bugs.reproduction: Manually corrupt recipe data so ingredients field is undefined, view RecipeDetail - app crashes
bugs.file_hint: RecipeDetailScreen.tsx:216-224
bugs.proposed_fix: Add array validation: `{(recipe.ingredients || []).map((ing, i) => ...`

bugs.category: Missing input length validation allowing DoS
bugs.attack_description: Email and password inputs in LoginScreen without maxLength property
bugs.expected: Inputs limited to reasonable length
bugs.actual: User can paste unlimited text (10MB+) into email/password fields, causing state bloat and potential crash
bugs.reproduction: In LoginScreen, paste 10MB of text into email field - app may lag or freeze
bugs.file_hint: LoginScreen.tsx:52-68
bugs.proposed_fix: Add maxLength properties: `<FormInput label='Email' maxLength={100} />` and password maxLength={64}

bugs.category: Potential double-submit due to slow state update
bugs.attack_description: Button disabled state depends on async loading flag that may not update fast enough
bugs.expected: Button is disabled immediately on press, preventing multiple submissions
bugs.actual: If user rapid double-clicks button, two login requests may be sent before loading state updates
bugs.reproduction: Rapid double-click login button - two simultaneous login requests fire to backend
bugs.file_hint: LoginScreen.tsx:75-76
bugs.proposed_fix: Add submitted flag: `const [submitted, setSubmitted] = useState(false); if (submitted) return; setSubmitted(true)`

bugs.category: Unchecked base64 image size causing storage overflow
bugs.attack_description: Image picker resizes but doesn't validate final base64 size before storing
bugs.expected: Base64 image size is validated and limited before storage
bugs.actual: pickImageFromLibrary compresses but base64 can still be 500KB+, exceeding AsyncStorage limits
bugs.reproduction: Select large image in avatar picker, save - may fail silently or with generic error
bugs.file_hint: ProfileScreen.tsx:25, RecipeFormScreen.tsx:57
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate base64 size: `if (picked.base64DataUri.length > 200000) { Alert.alert('Error', 'Image too large') }`

bugs.attack_description: Rendering user?.name in Text component without null fallback
bugs.expected: Render fallback text when user is null or loading
bugs.actual: During auth transition when user is null, renders 'undefined' text in ProfileScreen title
bugs.reproduction: Navigate to ProfileScreen during auth loading - see 'undefined' displayed
bugs.file_hint: ProfileScreen.tsx:128
bugs.proposed_fix: Add fallback: `<Text>{user?.name || 'Mi Perfil'}</Text>`

bugs.category: Event listener cleanup missing in complex component
bugs.attack_description: DraggableFlatList may create listeners without proper cleanup
bugs.expected: All event listeners cleaned up on unmount
bugs.actual: RecipeListScreen may leave drag listeners mounted, causing memory leak over repeated navigation
bugs.reproduction: Repeatedly navigate to/from RecipeListScreen 50+ times - memory increases
bugs.file_hint: RecipeListScreen.tsx:17-19
bugs.proposed_fix: Ensure DraggableFlatList cleanup in useEffect cleanup function

bugs.category: Async state update without mount check
bugs.attack_description: setSaving(false) called after saveRecipe promise even if component unmounts
bugs.expected: State updates only on mounted component
bugs.actual: If user closes modal or navigates during saveRecipe, setSaving called on unmounted component
bugs.reproduction: Open save modal, click save, immediately navigate back - React warning about state update
bugs.file_hint: RecipeDetailScreen.tsx:75-84
bugs.proposed_fix: Wrap setState in mount check: `if (isMountedRef.current) setSaving(false)`

bugs.attack_description: Recipe title truncated with numberOfLines={1} but may hide critical information
bugs.expected: User aware content is truncated
bugs.actual: Very long recipe titles silently truncated without clear visual feedback
bugs.reproduction: Create recipe with very long title - list view hides content without indication
bugs.file_hint: RecipeListScreen.tsx:119
bugs.proposed_fix: Add ellipsis or consider ExpandableText component for titles over 50 chars

bugs.category: Invalid data silently coerced without error feedback
bugs.attack_description: Invalid color value in GroupFormScreen silently replaced without notification
bugs.expected: User notified if group color is invalid
bugs.actual: If existing?.color is invalid, defaults to COLORS[0] silently
bugs.reproduction: Corrupt group color field in AsyncStorage, open GroupForm - shows default without error
bugs.file_hint: GroupFormScreen.tsx:31
bugs.proposed_fix: Validate and warn: add error state if color invalid

bugs.category: Expandable very long content causing performance issues
bugs.attack_description: ExpandableText with very long description causes potential lag
bugs.expected: Long text handled efficiently or limited
bugs.actual: recipe.description without hard truncation can cause scroll lag when expanded
bugs.reproduction: Create recipe with 10000+ char description, expand on RecipeDetail - UI lags
bugs.file_hint: RecipeDetailScreen.tsx:158-163
bugs.proposed_fix: Enforce maxLength=1000 on description display or truncate in render

bugs.category: Race condition with undefined array fallback
bugs.attack_description: Potential timing issue with recipe.groupIds fallback in filter operation
bugs.expected: groupIds safely defaults to empty array
bugs.actual: Edge case where recipe.groupIds could cause issue in useMemo filter operation
bugs.reproduction: Rapidly navigate with corrupted recipe data - potential race condition
bugs.file_hint: RecipeDetailScreen.tsx:28-31
bugs.proposed_fix: Explicit guard: `if (!recipe?.groupIds) return []` before filtering

summaries.summary: Se ejecutaron 50 ataques contra el backend deployado en Railway (https://recetario-app-backend-production.up.railway.app/api), enfocados en validación de entrada en los endpoints de autenticación, recetas y grupos. Se encontraron 31 bugs críticos e importantes de validación. La mayoría (21) son de tipo IMPORTANT, lo que significa que un profesor adversario puede explotarlos fácilmente para que la entrega falle. El ataque más peligroso es BUG-004 (CRITICAL) que permite insertar referencias a grupos inválidos, comprometiendo la integridad de datos. Los bugs incluyen: falta de validación de tipos (aceptar numbers en campos string, null en campos obligatorios, arrays como strings), falta de validación de rangos (números negativos/cero en servings, prepTime decimal), falta de sanitización de caracteres de control en strings (newlines, tabs), validación deficiente de formato en hex colors, y manejo incorrecto de códigos de estado HTTP (409 en lugar de 400). La arquitectura del backend carece de un esquema de validación centralizado, permitiendo que payload malformado sea aceptado y almacenado en la base de datos.

Executed 18 attack scenarios targeting authentication and cross-user authorization. Found 2 security bugs: (1) CRITICAL: Deleted user tokens remain valid indefinitely, allowing attackers to retain access to user accounts and resources even after deletion. (2) IMPORTANT: Reorder endpoints silently accept and ignore foreign resource IDs instead of validating ownership, creating inconsistent security behavior and potential for confusion/abuse. All other auth/authz controls (cross-user CRUD, private recipe access, Bearer validation) are functioning correctly. The two identified bugs represent gaps in access control enforcement at the API level.

Executed 23 coordinated attacks against backend cascade delete and referential integrity. Passed 20 tests, failed 3. Found 3 security/validation bugs: (1) 404 vs 403 leak on DELETE /groups/:id allows information disclosure about resource existence; (2) POST /recipes accepts non-existent groupIds, creating orphaned references; (3) PATCH /recipes/:id same issue - allows update with invalid groupIds. Most cascade operations (delete user, delete group with keepRecipes, delete recipe) work correctly and clean up SavedRecipes properly. Authorization checks for other users are mostly enforced. Main issues are missing input validation on groupIds and improper error codes leaking authorization info.

Encontré 8 bugs críticos e importantes durante pruebas de payload extremes y DoS:

BUGS DE VALIDACIÓN (3 críticos + 5 importantes):
- No hay límite en cantidad de ingredientes (aceptó 1000)
- No hay límite en cantidad de pasos (aceptó 1000)  
- No hay validación de tamaño de imagen (aceptó 2MB+1)
- No hay validación de formato de imagen data URI (aceptó URIs malformados)
- No hay validación de base64 válido en imágenes (aceptó caracteres inválidos)

BUGS DE PÉRDIDA DE DATOS (2 importantes):
- El campo quantity se trunca silenciosamente a 10 caracteres
- El campo unit se trunca silenciosamente a 20 caracteres

BUG DE CRASH/DoS (1 crítico):
- Payloads de 10MB en description causan error 500 (crash del servidor)

RESULTADOS DE ATAQUES: 8/15 pasaron, 7/15 fallaron detectando los bugs anteriores.

El backend tiene fallas severas en validación de entrada que un profesor explotaría fácilmente para romper la entrega.

summaries.area: Backend API validation, data consistency, and input handling
summaries.summary: Ejecuté 16 ataques contra el backend deployado en Railway. El sistema tiene BUENA protección contra acceso no autorizado (auth, ownership checks funcionan) y buena manejo de cascadas (deletes de grupos/recetas). Sin embargo, encontré 5 bugs importantes: (1) no valida que prepTime sea >= 0, (2) no valida que servings sea > 0, (3) acepta reorder con IDs duplicados sin error, (4) silencia falla de groupIds inválidos en saves (inconsistencia), (5) no sanitiza inputs XSS en títulos/descripciones. Estos son problemas de validación input, no de seguridad crítica, pero afectan calidad y pueden causar UX roto o datos inválidos.

Analisis defensivo de RecetasApp frontend encontro 15 bugs potenciales: 3 CRITICOS que causan crashes (unsafe author access, Image URI rendering, state updates on unmount), 6 IMPORTANTES con validacion faltante (maxLength inputs, double-submit, image size overflow, async unmount), y 6 MINOR con problemas de UX/perf. El codigo carece de defensas contra inputs grandes, corrupted data del backend, race conditions en async operations, y proper null-checking en accesos de propiedades. Profesor puede explotar: pegar 10MB en login fields, crear recipes con undefined fields, rapidamente navegar durante saves, corromper AsyncStorage. Severidad global: ALTA - multiples crash vectors identificados."
